mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference vs baseline is insignificant with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital goods demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive 60-day return in > 50% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and rising input costs that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in > 55% of observations or the mechanism shows no predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points in a single month signals aggressive monetary accommodation that compresses discount rates and triggers immediate positive S&P 500 repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 65% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive significance when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market deterioration and falling aggregate demand, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward testing with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger macroeconomic activity and corporate revenue expansion, which directly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns with p > 0.05 or positive return frequency drops below 50% in high-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates weak manufacturing demand and delayed economic normalization, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in recovery phases yield positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases or the signal out-of-sample accuracy falls below random chance over a 2-year rolling window.
